title: 5 Enrolling in PaymentStream AFT
body_text:
5	Enrolling in PaymentStream AFT
5.1	Overview of Enrolling in PaymentStream AFT
The Originator can be a financial institution or a business member of the financial institution. If your financial institution is the Originator, you can originate AFT transactions for individual clients (e.g., for mortgage payments, loan payments) or for your own purposes (e.g., payroll, investments).
If your business member is the Originator, your financial institution can generate AFT files on the business memberâs behalf, or the business member can generate its own AFT files. Business members that generate their own AFT files can either enter the transactions on
PaymentStreamâ¢
AFT or they can upload AFT files to
PaymentStream
AFT.
All Originators must be set up on the
PaymentStream
Direct platform to implement
PaymentStream
AFT. Contact Central 1 Support to enroll your financial institution in
PaymentStream
Direct. See the
PaymentStream
Direct Part for more information.
It may take up to two weeks from the date Central 1 receives the appropriate forms and agreements from the Originator to process a
PaymentStream
AFT set-up request. Delays can occur if the forms and agreements are completed incorrectly or arenât completed in full.
The forms and agreements required for enrollment in
PaymentStream
AFT are determined by:
Which party is the Originator (your financial institution or your business member)
The AFT transaction type (PAD and/or direct deposit)
The type of PAD that will be created, if applicable (e.g., Cash Management PADs)
See the Introduction to AFT Part for procedures on completing the necessary forms and agreements.
Additional Originator IDs for Business Members
Some business members may request to have multiple AFT Originator IDs set up that are associated with the same business member company. In this instance, the settlement reports for all associated Originator IDs are combined in one report folder for the business members to access through
PaymentStream
AFT. There is a reduced implementation fee for additional Originator IDs since the business member company, their access, and their report folder have already been set up.
Expiry Dates for Originator IDs
Central 1 deletes Originator IDs that are inactive on
PaymentStream
AFT for more than 12 months.
5.2	Requesting PaymentStream AFT Service Types
If the Originator is a business member, the Originatorâs financial institution works with the member and Central 1 to set up AFT service. Your financial institution determines if the business member or your financial institution will enter the AFT transactions on
PaymentStream
AFT.
The Originator chooses from the following
PaymentStream
AFT service types:
File Upload
â The Originator creates AFT files using in-house or third-party software and then uploads the files to
PaymentStream
AFT. Files uploaded to
PaymentStream
AFT must conform to Central 1âs AFT file specifications. See Central 1âs AFT File Specifications, Form 2726, for details. These specifications are based on CPA Standard 005, Standards for the Exchange of Financial Data on AFT Files, with some modifications. See
PaymentStream
AFT â Guide for File Upload, Form 5600, for procedures.
Data Entry Automatic Release
â The Originator enters AFT transactions on
PaymentStream
AFT and the system releases them automatically. See
PaymentStream
AFT â Guide for Automatic Release, Form 5598, for procedures.
Data Entry Manual Release
â The Originator enters AFT transactions on
PaymentStream
AFT and then releases the transactions manually. See
PaymentStream
AFT â Guide for Manual Release, Form 5599, for procedures.
The Originator determines if users require approval for tasks performed on
PaymentStream
AFT. See Chapter 12, Approval of Tasks/Transactions, for more information. Approvals are mandatory for all users when a financial institution or business member has one or more Originator IDs and the monthly accumulative limit is one million dollars or more. See Chapter 14, Managing Risk on
PaymentStream
AFT, for more information.
The Originator completes the forms and agreements as described in the Introduction to AFT Part. Central 1 sets up all Originators and advises your financial institution when the set-up is complete.
Your financial institution adds users and permissions on User Management and advises the Originator when the users are set up. For procedures on setting up AFT users and assigning permissions, see the User Management Part.
Your financial institution then follows internal processes to provide applicable documentation to the business member.
Related Information
Central 1âs AFT File Specifications,
Form 2726
PaymentStream
AFT â Guide for Automatic Release,
Form 5598
PaymentStream
AFT â Guide for Manual Release,
Form 5599
PaymentStream
AFT â Guide for File Upload,
Form 5600
5.3	Providing 2-Step Security to Business Members
Business members must be set up with 2-step security to access
PaymentStream
AFT. 2-step security access is managed by CPS Admin Security Officers via User Management. Users can be assigned a hard token or a soft token. See the User Management Part for procedures on assigning 2-step security tokens to users.
Hard tokens are distributed to users after the CPS Admin Security Officer assigns access to the users on User Management. Soft tokens require an additional step. After the CPS Admin Security Officer assigns access to the user on User Management, the soft token user must register and install the soft token on their mobile device within 14 days. For more information on security tokens, see the 2-Step Security Part.
The
PaymentStream
AFT â 2-Step Security Access for Business Members, Form 6011, provides procedures on registering and installing soft tokens. Financial institutions can distribute this user guide to their business members.
Related Information
PaymentStream
AFT â 2-Step Security Access for Business Members,
Form 6011
5.4	AFT File Specifications
AFT files uploaded to
PaymentStream
AFT must comply with Central 1âs file specifications. These specifications are based on CPA Standard 005, Standards for the Exchange of Financial Data on AFT Files, with some modifications. Files with an invalid format will be rejected at Central 1. See Central 1âs AFT File Specifications, Form 2726, for details.
Related Information
Central 1âs AFT File Specifications,
Form 2726
END
